We are seeking a **Senior Data Scientist** to design, train, evaluate, and deliver machine learning models that solve operational problems across USCENTCOM’s Data Office initiatives. This is a hands-on ML practitioner role—not a platform or infrastructure position. The Senior Data Scientist will work within an established on-premises Data Analytical Environment (DAE) built on a Data Lakehouse architecture with H100 GPU infrastructure, applying their expertise in statistical modeling, deep learning, and applied ML to turn enterprise data into actionable intelligence. The ideal candidate brings deep experience in model development across multiple problem domains—forecasting, NLP, anomaly detection, and classification—and can independently lead the ML practice for the team.
...
* **Design, train, and validate **supervised, unsupervised, and deep learning models using open-source libraries (PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM) to support forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, and NLP use cases

* **Fine-tune and adapt open-source LLMs **(LLMA, Mistral, and similar) for domain-specific tasks including document summarization, entity extraction, and question-answering over classified and unclassified networks

...

### 1. What is a AI Engineer at General Dynamics Mission Systems?
...
At **General Dynamics Mission Systems**, an **AI Engineer**—ranging from mid-level practitioners to the **Chief Engineer – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Applications**—plays a pivotal role in designing, deploying, and securing cutting-edge technologies that protect national security. Operating at the intersection of defense technology and advanced data science, these professionals build cyber-resilient, highly reliable AI solutions for the Department of Defense (DoD), government laboratories, and allied military forces. The systems you develop and architect directly impact warfighter readiness, maritime intelligence, and strategic defense infrastructure across the globe.
...
Working in this position means solving some of the most complex engineering challenges in the industry. Unlike consumer-facing AI roles, an **AI Engineer** at **General Dynamics Mission Systems** deals with highly sensitive, multi-modal datasets—including signal intelligence, cybersecurity telemetry, natural language processing, and high-resolution imagery—often in resource-constrained edge environments. You will be responsible for translating abstract mission requirements into robust machine learning pipelines that can operate under extreme conditions where failure is not an option.

# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...

Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
